name: Crow Flock
description: Intelligent scavengers. Not a threat — an omen. Where crows gather, something's dead or dying.

sensory.sight: black shapes against grey sky, circling, circling, landing in a tree to watch
sensory.sound: cawing — conversational, argumentative, occasionally mimicking human sounds
sensory.smell: nothing from the crows — but follow them and you'll find what they're eating
sensory.feel: the unsettling awareness that they're watching you specifically

encounter_hooks: a column of crows rising from behind a hill — something's there
a single crow following the party, hopping from tree to tree
crows abandoning an area suddenly — whatever scared them is worse than you
notes: Crows are living atmosphere. They signal death, danger, and carrion. A flock circling overhead is a navigation tool — follow them to find bodies, battlefields, or Fray-touched areas where animals have died. In Threadhallow, crows avoid Frayed zones (they can sense the weave instability).
